text.title: Cheap-model tripwire short-circuits the expensive agent
text.summary: Run a small/fast classifier as an input_guardrail BEFORE the main agent; a tripwire verdict raises synchronously, so the strong model and its tools never execute on filtered traffic.

section.title: The rule and the wire-up
section.p: An input guardrail is one constrained-output call to a cheap model that returns a typed verdict. The SDK wraps the call so that `tripwire_triggered=True` raises `InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered` and the main agent's expensive loop never starts. This is a control-flow short circuit, not a routing decision — there is no fallback path on tripwire, only a structured rejection.

rule.statement: For every public-facing agent on a premium model, attach at least one input_guardrail that runs gpt-4o-mini or Haiku-class with `text_format` set to a Pydantic verdict (`{is_offtopic, is_jailbreak, is_abuse, reason}`); set `tripwire_triggered = is_offtopic or is_jailbreak or is_abuse`.
rule.rationale: OpenAI Agents SDK runs guardrails before the agent loop; a tripwire exception is the only deterministic way to guarantee zero tokens and zero tool side-effects on filtered requests. Without it, prompts like "ignore previous instructions" reach the strong model and at minimum cost an Opus turn.

example.code:
from agents import Agent, GuardrailFunctionOutput, Runner, input_guardrail from pydantic import BaseModel
class Verdict(BaseModel): is_offtopic: bool is_jailbreak: bool reason: str
screener = Agent(name="screener", model="gpt-4o-mini", instructions="Classify the user message.", output_type=Verdict)
@input_guardrail async def gate(ctx, agent, msg) -> GuardrailFunctionOutput: res = await Runner.run(screener, msg) v: Verdict = res.final_output return GuardrailFunctionOutput( output_info=v, tripwire_triggered=v.is_offtopic or v.is_jailbreak, )
main = Agent(name="main", model="gpt-5", input_guardrails=[gate], tools=[...])
example.note: Cheap model returns a typed verdict; tripwire raises before any gpt-5 token is generated; main agent's tool side-effects cannot fire on filtered traffic.

example.code: # Anti-pattern: classify INSIDE the main agent's first tool call main = Agent(name="main", model="gpt-5", tools=[classify, do_work]) # Strong model burns at least one turn deciding whether to run classify; # even on a "no, this is spam" verdict you have already paid for an # Opus/gpt-5 turn and risked the model skipping classify entirely.
example.note: Classification inside the agent loop costs strong-model tokens on every request and gives the model the option to skip the gate.

section.reference: Working OpenAI Agents SDK guardrail with Pydantic verdict and cheap classifier.
